{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided in the documents, serum glutamic oxaloacetic transaminase (SGOT or AST) is not specifically mentioned as being increased in renal failure, prostatic carcinoma, or pulmonary edema. However, it is mentioned that AST can be elevated in hepatic congestion secondary to heart failure. Since myocardial infarction is associated with elevated liver enzymes, including AST, it is reasonable to conclude that serum glutamic oxaloacetic transaminase is increased in myocardial infarction.", "answer_choice": "C"}
